description: US federal tax credits for low-income individuals and families: EITC, CTC, Saver's Credit, premium tax credits, and free filing via VITA/TCE. Reflects tax year 2025 parameters.
source: IRS Publication 596 (EITC), 972 (CTC), 590-A (Saver's Credit), 974 (premium tax credits), VITA program; verified 2026

triples.subject: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
triples.object: refundable federal tax credit for low-to-moderate income workers; one of largest anti-poverty programs

triples.subject: EITC 2025
triples.object: $7,830 for families with 3 or more qualifying children

triples.subject: EITC 2025
triples.object: $6,960 for families with 2 qualifying children

triples.subject: EITC 2025
triples.object: $4,213 for families with 1 qualifying child

triples.subject: EITC 2025
triples.object: $632 for workers without qualifying children (ages 25–64)

triples.subject: EITC
triples.object: must have earned income, valid SSN, meet income limits, and not be claimed as dependent

triples.subject: EITC
triples.object: investment income must be $11,600 or less per year

triples.subject: EITC
triples.object: yes — if credit exceeds taxes owed, IRS pays the difference as a refund

triples.subject: Child Tax Credit (CTC)
triples.object: up to $2,000 per qualifying child under age 17 as of 2025 tax year

triples.subject: Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC)
triples.object: refundable portion of CTC — up to $1,700 per child for 2025 if credit exceeds tax liability

triples.subject: Child Tax Credit
triples.object: begins phasing out at $200,000 AGI (single) or $400,000 (married filing jointly)

triples.subject: Child Tax Credit
triples.object: under 17 at end of tax year, related to taxpayer, lived with taxpayer over half year, SSN required

triples.subject: Saver's Credit
triples.object: Retirement Savings Contributions Credit — 26 U.S.C. § 25B

triples.subject: Saver's Credit
triples.object: credit of 10–50% of retirement contributions to IRA or employer plan, up to $2,000 ($4,000 MFJ)

triples.subject: Saver's Credit 2025
triples.object: AGI at or below $38,250 for single filers

triples.subject: Saver's Credit 2025
triples.object: AGI at or below $76,500 for married filing jointly

triples.subject: Saver's Credit
triples.object: not refundable — only reduces tax owed; claim on IRS Form 8880

triples.subject: Premium Tax Credit
triples.object: subsidy for health insurance purchased through ACA marketplace for households 100–400% FPL (no cliff as of 2025)

triples.subject: Premium Tax Credit
triples.object: 26 U.S.C. § 36B; ARP 2021 expanded eligibility and IRA 2022 extended through 2025

triples.subject: Premium Tax Credit
triples.object: can be taken in advance (APTC) to lower monthly premiums, reconciled at tax time on Form 8962

triples.subject: Premium Tax Credit
triples.object: report changes in income or household to marketplace within 30 days to avoid repayment at tax time

triples.subject: VITA
triples.object: Volunteer Income Tax Assistance — IRS-sponsored free tax prep for income under ~$67,000

triples.subject: VITA
triples.object: IRS.gov/vita or call 800-906-9887 for nearest site; also available at many libraries and community centers

triples.subject: TCE
triples.object: Tax Counseling for the Elderly — free tax prep for age 60+, specializes in pension and retirement questions

triples.subject: TCE
triples.object: AARP Tax-Aide locator at aarp.org/money/taxes/aarp_taxaide; available Feb–April

triples.subject: IRS Free File
triples.object: free federal tax software for AGI at or below $84,000 (2025); Free File Fillable Forms available to all

triples.subject: IRS Free File
triples.object: irs.gov/freefile — access only through IRS website to avoid upsell traps

triples.subject: Child and Dependent Care Credit
triples.object: credit for childcare or dependent care expenses needed to work; 20–35% of up to $3,000 (1 child) or $6,000 (2+)

triples.subject: Child and Dependent Care Credit
triples.object: nonrefundable federally (some states have refundable versions)

triples.subject: tax filing tip
triples.object: if income dropped significantly, you can elect to use prior year earned income to calculate EITC if it yields a higher credit
